title: Hotjar API credentials should not be disclosed
description: Hotjar is a behavior analytics platform offering heatmaps, session recordings, and user surveys. Hotjar credentials include site IDs and API keys. Exposing these allows access to session recordings that may contain sensitive user interactions and form inputs.
recommendation: Remove Hotjar credentials from source code. Store in environment variables. Rotate at insights.hotjar.com.
impact: Unauthorized access to session recordings (which may contain passwords and PII entered by users), heatmap data, and user survey responses.

checks.message: Hotjar API key detected in variable assignment. Hotjar API keys provide access to session recordings that may contain users' sensitive interactions (form inputs, passwords, credit card numbers), heatmap data, survey responses, and user behavioral analytics. This represents a significant privacy risk. Rotate immediately at insights.hotjar.com and review data access logs.
